Description: Set the server's starting map. Enter a stock or custom map name.
Set the server's starting scenario. Pick a stock scenario or select Enter Custom and type your own scenario name below. Not all scenarios work with all maps.
Sets a custom scenario to use for Starting Scenario, if Enter Custom is selected.
Set the server's starting lighting. Must be set to Day if no map and Scenario are chosen.
Set the server's starting game mode. Must be set to None/Random if no map and Scenario are chosen.
Set the servers hostname
The actual startup command line passed to Sandstorm.
The stock startup command line AMP will use when 'Use Default Startup Command Line' is enabled. Edit this if you want to change the built-in default.
Your custom override startup command line. Uncheck 'Use Default Startup Command Line' to use this value, or re-check it to go back to the default above.
Set server maximum amount of players
Set the steam GSLTToken APPID 581320 [Get A GSLToken ID](https://steamcommunity.com/dev/managegameservers/)
Set the mod.io server access token for this game. AMP writes this value into GameUserSettings.ini so Sandstorm can authenticate mod downloads.
Set the game stats token for XP Gain [Get A GameStats Token ID](https://gamestats.sandstorm.game/)
Sets a password for the server
See the Security and Privacy menu for configurable options for the server's RCON
Combined mutator list used on startup. It is built from the checkboxes below plus any custom mutators you type.
Start with 100 supply points.
Only anti-materiel rifles are available along with normal equipment and explosives.
Only bolt-action rifles are available along with normal equipment and explosives.
Start with 0 supply points.
Start with 3 supply points.
Health is increased.
Equipment is more expensive, rounds are shorter, and capturing objectives is faster.
Player classes are replaced with those from Competitive.
Move faster.
All weapons, equipment, and explosives in the game are available in the Loadout Menu.
Fight against AI enemies who only use melee attacks. Watch out for special enemies.
Start with 5 supply points.
Kill the enemy more than they kill you with a big stinkin' revolver. And explosives.
Mutator featuring slower movement speeds and longer capture times.
Players only take damage when shot in the head.
A live fragmentation grenade is dropped on death.
Weapons always point to the center of the screen.
Makarovs are the only option.
Aiming down sights is disabled.
No scavenging allowed.
Only pistols are available along with normal equipment and explosives.
Start with limited supply.
Only shotguns are available along with normal equipment and explosives.
Objectives will take longer to capture.
Move slower.
Gain supply points as your score increases.
Start with 30 supply points.
Start with 1 supply point.
Everyone dies with one shot.
Receive health when dealing damage to enemies equal to the amount of damage dealt.
Start with 10 supply points.
Set the rules for the server
Enables the server to use a Modlist
Toggle Easy Anti-Cheat on or off. Leave enabled for normal public play; use the off setting only if you explicitly want a no-EAC server.
Combined mod travel string used on startup. It uses the stock preset below unless you switch to your custom override.
Checked = use the stock mod-map travel string shown below. Unchecked = use the custom travel string.
The stock mod-map travel string AMP will use when 'Use Default Mod Travel To' is enabled.
Your custom mod-map travel string. Uncheck 'Use Default Mod Travel To' to use this value, or re-check it to return to the preset above.
Set the server MOTD
A list of Steam64 IDs for players that are in-game admins. [Find your Steam64 ID](https://steamid.io/)
Enables cheat commands on the server
Add one map cycle entry per line. Reorder entries in the list editor to control rotation. The defaults are a checkpoint rotation for stock and community maps.
Preloaded with the map pack you listed. Add, remove, and reorder one workshop ID at a time in the list editor.
Is the kill feed enabled?
Is the kill feed enabled for dedicated spectators and replays?
Show information about a death (killer, weapon) to the victim?
Reveal distance of kills?
Limit for the number of team kills one player can perform before being kicked.
Grace timer between team killing incidents.
How long until a player’s TK count reduces by 1.
Can alive players see messages from the dead?

DisplayName: Can the dead speak to teammates?
Description: Can alive players see messages from dead teammates using team-only chat?
Can alive players hear voice chat from dead players?

DisplayName: Allow enemies to hear proximity chat?
Description: Can proximity voice chat be heard by enemies?

DisplayName: Allow radio chat to broadcast on local channel
Description: Can radio chat also broadcast on the local channel?
How long it takes to capture territorial objectives.
How long objective progress decays with no one on the point. Negative value disables.
Additional capture speed-up for each player on the point.
Maximum players taken into account for capture speed-up.

DisplayName: Kill Feed Game Starting Intermission time
Description: How long after a round starts before showing a kill feed
The additional time to wait for additional players during the starting intermission.
How long players are frozen before transitioning to the round intermission screen.
How long is spent on the intermission screen.
How much time is spent between last post round and game over.
Should teams be assigned on connect?
Enables friendly fire.
Damage modifier for friendly fire
Damage modifier for friendly fire that is mirrored back at the attacker.
Enables auto team balancing.
How long to wait after detecting unbalanced teams.
Enabled map voting at the end of each game.
Enables the use of the map cycle. If disabled, the current map loops indefinitely.
Allow both teams to talk to each other between rounds.
How long a player can be idle for before being kicked.
How long a player can idle for when there are low reinforcements.
How frequently to check for idle players.
Can Players use 3rd person spectator mode?

DisplayName: Minimum Players to start the game.
Description: Minimum players required on each team to start the game.
Maximum number of rounds to play.
Number of rounds required for game victory.

DisplayName: Round Won Once Win Limit Reached
Description: When enabled, the server advances after a round is won once the win limit is reached.
Number of rounds required for game victory.
Amount of “freeze time” at the start of a round.
Duration of the round.
Amount of overtime given when there is an objective being contested after the round timer expires.
Minimum additional time spent between team switches.
How often to switch teams. 0 = never, 1 = every round, 2 = every other round, etc.
Allow players to switch to the other team?
Enables Bots
Bot quota to fill.
Enemy count used for solo checkpoint scaling.
Minimum enemy count for checkpoint scaling.
Maximum enemy count for checkpoint scaling.

DisplayName: Max Players To Scale Enemy Count
Description: Maximum player count used when scaling enemy counts.
Amount of starting supply points.
Maximum supply that can be earned.
Enable supply gain throughout the round.
Should supply gain be awarded instantly?
Score threshold for earning supply.
